title: The Lapahn Attack
description: On pages 5–8, the lapahn attack as luffy refuses to fight the wildlife while carrying Nami, and the journey toward the doctor becomes a brutal climb through Drum’s snowfields.
scene: The Lapahn Attack. Luffy refuses to fight the wildlife while carrying Nami, and the journey toward the doctor becomes a brutal climb through Drum’s snowfields. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I guess it's expected of a snow creature,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: to be able to move this swiftly over thick snow...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Luffy! Don't lay a hand on any of them!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But the impact of every blow you deliver or receive,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: will be trans- Ferred over to Nami as well!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Damn it... hard to get any good kicks,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: With both feet stuck in deep snow...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: I'll cover you so run to the forest!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: We have to shake them off somehow. If we were to seriously fight all of them, we'd be stuck here until the sun goes down!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I told you to leave them to me!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This is a matter of life and death!

summary: This part centers on the lapahn attack, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
